gaps: Existing vendor APIs expose raw HTTP surfaces; this print packages command discovery, agent defaults, local sync/search, SQL analytics, and MCP access.
patterns: Read-first GTM and support workflows benefit from compact JSON, --agent defaults, local SQLite mirrors, and deterministic command discovery.

novel_features.name: Local SQLite mirror
novel_features.description: Sync read endpoints into a local SQLite store for offline search, joins, and repeatable agent analysis.
novel_features.rationale: Agent-native CLI affordance shipped in this print.

novel_features.name: Full-text search across API data
novel_features.description: Search live or locally synced API records from one agent-friendly command.
novel_features.rationale: Agent-native CLI affordance shipped in this print.

novel_features.name: SQL analytics on synced records
novel_features.description: Run read-only SQL against the local store for compound questions the upstream API does not expose directly.
novel_features.rationale: Agent-native CLI affordance shipped in this print.

novel_features.name: Agent command discovery
novel_features.description: Map natural-language capabilities to the exact CLI command path before spending tokens on trial and error.
novel_features.rationale: Agent-native CLI affordance shipped in this print.

narrative.headline: Calendly scheduling reads and availability context for agents.
narrative.value_prop: Wraps Calendly read APIs with agent defaults, local SQLite sync, search, analytics, command discovery, and MCP tools so agents can inspect scheduling context without hand-building HTTP calls.

quickstart.comment: Inspect the auth and connectivity diagnostic surface.
Review how to build the local SQLite mirror before offline analysis.
Find the exact command path for a natural-language task.
